routines.description: Create a focused briefing from the selected project or conversation context for a specific day.
routines.name: Morning brief

parameters.description: The day covered by the briefing.
parameters.label: Day
parameters.name: day

default.value: Priorities, deadlines, blockers, and decisions that need attention.

routines.prompt_template: Prepare a concise morning brief for {{day}}. Focus on {{focus}}. Use only context and tools available to this project or conversation, distinguish facts from inference, and call out missing sources rather than inventing updates.
routines.summary: Summarise priorities, deadlines, blockers, and decisions for the day.

routines.description: Run a structured, multi-agent review of a pull request and optionally prepare a GitHub review for publication.
routines.name: Pull request review

parameters.description: The GitHub pull request to review.
parameters.label: Pull request
parameters.name: pull_request

parameters.description: Exactly two provider/model combinations that independently review the pull request.
parameters.label: Reviewers
parameters.name: reviewers

parameters.description: The review dimensions each reviewer should assess.
parameters.label: Review dimensions
parameters.name: dimensions

parameters.description: Additional review emphasis, context, or constraints.
parameters.label: Extra instructions
parameters.name: extra_instructions

parameters.description: Post the synthesised review comments back to GitHub after review.
parameters.label: Post review comments
parameters.name: post_comments

routines.prompt_template:
Review pull request {{pull_request}}.

Run exactly two independent reviewer sessions using these provider/model selections: {{reviewers}}.
Review dimensions: {{dimensions}}.
Extra instructions: {{extra_instructions}}.
Post the synthesised review comments to GitHub: {{post_comments}}.

Use the project orchestration tools and preserve the pull request's repository context. Require evidence from both reviewers before synthesising. Do not claim that comments were posted unless the publication tool confirms it.
routines.summary: Review a pull request with independent agents and a synthesised verdict.

routines.description: Inspect open issues in a selected repository, group related reports, and propose a prioritised triage plan.
routines.name: Issue triage

parameters.description: The repository whose issues should be triaged.
parameters.label: Repository
parameters.name: repository

parameters.description: The classifications to include in the triage pass.
parameters.label: Triage dimensions
parameters.name: dimensions

parameters.description: Prepare proposed labels and comments, but never publish without an explicit approved tool action.
parameters.label: Prepare updates
parameters.name: prepare_updates

routines.prompt_template: Triage open issues for {{repository}} across {{dimensions}}. Prepare updates: {{prepare_updates}}. Group duplicates with evidence, identify missing reproduction details, and propose priorities. Do not publish labels or comments unless an authorised tool confirms the write.
routines.summary: Categorise open issues, identify duplicates, and propose priorities.

routines.description: Check configured Jarvis workers and services for availability, degraded state, and actionable failures.
routines.name: System health check

parameters.description: Optional worker selection; empty checks all workers visible to the project.
parameters.label: Workers
parameters.name: workers

parameters.description: Health signals included in the check.
parameters.label: Checks
parameters.name: checks

routines.prompt_template: Run a system health check using these checks: {{checks}}. Worker selection: {{workers}}; an empty list means all workers visible to the project. Report healthy, degraded, and unavailable components separately. Prefer observed runtime state and recent events; do not expose secrets or private host details.
routines.summary: Check worker availability, capacity, sessions, and recent failures.

routines.description: Draft release notes from repository history with explicit audience and release range.
routines.name: Draft release notes

parameters.description: The repository whose changes should be summarised.
parameters.label: Repository
parameters.name: repository

parameters.description: Optional tag, branch, or commit range. Empty uses the repository's documented release convention.
parameters.label: Release range
parameters.name: release_range

parameters.description: The primary audience for the notes.
parameters.label: Audience
parameters.name: audience

routines.prompt_template: Draft release notes for {{repository}} over range {{release_range}} for {{audience}}. Derive claims from commits, pull requests, and repository release guidance. Separate breaking changes and operator actions. Do not publish a release, create tags, or modify release metadata.
routines.summary: Draft evidence-backed release notes without publishing a release.
